name: Data Models
description: Pure data-definition module providing Pydantic models and type aliases that define the shared data structures for the entire Constrain application. Zero internal project imports — serves as the leaf dependency for all other components. Includes: Posture (StrEnum), Phase/Status/Severity (Literal type aliases), FailureMode, ProblemModel (with incremental update), Message, Constraint, LLMResponseMeta (with safe parsing), and Session (full state container with serialization). All file I/O is excluded; serialization stops at string boundaries.

types.name: Posture
types.description: Interview posture that controls system prompt tone. Implemented as Python enum.StrEnum for clean serialization and CLI integration.

types.name: Phase
types.description: Type alias: Literal['understand', 'challenge', 'synthesize', 'complete']. Constrains session progression through the interview pipeline.

types.name: Status
types.description: Type alias: Literal['incomplete', 'complete']. Tracks whether a session has produced final artifacts.

types.name: Severity
types.description: Type alias: Literal['must', 'should']. Constraint severity level indicating hard requirement vs recommendation.

types.name: Role
types.description: Type alias: Literal['user', 'assistant']. Conversation message role.

types.name: FailureMode

fields.name: description
fields.description: Human-readable description of the failure mode.

fields.name: severity
fields.description: Severity classification of this failure mode (e.g., 'critical', 'major', 'minor').

fields.name: historical
fields.description: Whether this failure mode has been observed historically.

types.description: A typed failure mode replacing raw dict. Provides validation at the LLM output boundary. Represents a way the system under discussion can fail.

types.name: ProblemModel

fields.name: system_description
fields.description: High-level description of the system under discussion.

fields.name: stakeholders
fields.description: People or roles affected by or interested in the system.

fields.name: failure_modes
fields.description: Ways the system can fail, each as a typed FailureMode.

fields.name: dependencies
fields.description: External systems, services, or components this system depends on.

fields.name: assumptions
fields.description: Assumptions made about the system or its environment.

fields.name: boundaries
fields.description: Explicit boundaries or scope limitations of the system.

fields.name: history
fields.description: Relevant historical context or past decisions.

fields.name: success_shape
fields.description: What success looks like — observable outcomes or criteria.

types.description: Accumulated understanding of the problem being constrained. All fields have empty defaults so instances can be incrementally built. Supports shallow merge via apply_update: scalars are replaced, lists are extended with dedup for str lists, and unknown keys are silently ignored.

types.name: Message

fields.name: role
fields.description: Who produced this message: 'user' or 'assistant'.

fields.name: content
fields.description: The textual content of the message.

validators.error_message: Message content must not be empty.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the message was created. Default factory: datetime.now(timezone.utc).

types.description: A single conversation turn in the interview session. Timestamp defaults to UTC now at creation time.

types.name: Constraint

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for this constraint (e.g., 'C-001').

validators.error_message: Constraint id must not be empty.

fields.name: boundary
fields.description: The domain boundary or area this constraint applies to.

fields.name: condition
fields.description: The specific condition that must hold or should hold.

fields.name: violation
fields.description: What constitutes a violation of this constraint.

fields.name: severity
fields.description: Whether this is a hard requirement ('must') or recommendation ('should').

fields.name: rationale
fields.description: Why this constraint exists — the reasoning behind it.

types.description: A structured constraint extracted from the interview. Designed for YAML-safe export via model_dump(mode='json').

types.name: Artifacts

fields.name: prompt_md
fields.description: Generated prompt.md content as a string.

fields.name: constraints_yaml
fields.description: Generated constraints.yaml content as a string.

types.description: Container for generated output artifact content strings. Populated during synthesis phase.

types.name: LLMResponseMeta

fields.name: ready_to_proceed
fields.description: Whether the LLM considers the current phase complete and ready to advance.

fields.name: problem_model_update
fields.description: Incremental updates to apply to the ProblemModel. Keys correspond to ProblemModel field names; values are replacement scalars or items to append to lists.

types.description: Parsed structured JSON block from the end of LLM responses during understand/challenge phases. Defaults are safe fallbacks (not ready, no updates). Includes safe_parse classmethod that never raises.

types.name: Session

fields.name: id
fields.description: UUID4 string uniquely identifying this session. Default factory: str(uuid4()).

fields.name: schema_version
fields.description: Schema version for forward compatibility of persisted session files.

validators.error_message: Schema version must be at least 1.

fields.name: posture
fields.description: Interview posture controlling system prompt tone. Stored in session state but not displayed to the user.

fields.name: current_phase
fields.description: Current phase of the interview pipeline.

fields.name: current_round
fields.description: Current round number within the active phase. Starts at 0, incremented each turn.

fields.name: conversation_history
fields.description: Ordered list of all conversation turns across all phases.

fields.name: problem_model
fields.description: Incrementally accumulated understanding of the problem.

fields.name: artifacts
fields.description: Generated artifacts, populated only after successful synthesis. None before synthesis completes.

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of session creation. Default factory: datetime.now(timezone.utc).

fields.name: updated_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of last session modification. Updated by touch(). Default factory: datetime.now(timezone.utc).

fields.name: status
fields.description: Overall session completion status.

types.description: Full session state container. Represents the complete state of an interview session including conversation history, problem model, phase tracking, and optional generated artifacts. Provides to_json/from_json for serialization to .constrain/sessions/<id>.json. Includes schema_version for forward compatibility.

types.name: OptionalArtifacts
types.description: Optional wrapper for Artifacts — None before synthesis, populated after.

functions.name: ProblemModel.apply_update
functions.description: Apply an incremental update dict to the problem model using shallow merge semantics. Scalar fields (str) are replaced. List fields are extended: str lists are deduplicated, FailureMode lists are appended without dedup. Keys not present in ProblemModel.model_fields are silently ignored (suitable for debug logging if added later). Uses model_fields introspection to determine field types and merge behavior.

inputs.name: update
inputs.description: Dict of incremental updates. Keys are ProblemModel field names. Values are replacement values for scalars or items/lists to append for list fields.

error_cases.name: invalid_scalar_type
error_cases.condition: A scalar field update value is not a str

error_data.message: Expected str for scalar field '{field_name}', got {type}

error_cases.name: invalid_list_value_type
error_cases.condition: A list field update value is neither a list nor a single item of the expected type

error_data.message: Expected list or single item for list field '{field_name}', got {type}

error_cases.name: invalid_failure_mode_item
error_cases.condition: An item in a failure_modes update cannot be validated as a FailureMode

preconditions: update is a dict (may be empty)
self is a valid ProblemModel instance
postconditions: For each key in update that matches a scalar ProblemModel field, self.<key> equals update[key]
For each key in update that matches a list[str] ProblemModel field, the new items from update[key] are appended and the list contains no duplicate strings
For each key in update that matches a list[FailureMode] field, the new FailureMode items are appended
Keys in update not present in ProblemModel.model_fields are silently ignored
Pre-existing list items are preserved in original order
An empty update dict results in no changes

structured_side_effects.description: Mutates fields of the ProblemModel instance in place.

functions.name: LLMResponseMeta.safe_parse
functions.description: Classmethod that safely parses a raw dict into an LLMResponseMeta instance. Wraps model_validate in try/except and returns a safe default (ready_to_proceed=False, problem_model_update={}) on any validation failure. Never raises.

inputs.name: raw
inputs.description: Raw dict parsed from the JSON block in an LLM response. May be malformed, missing keys, or contain unexpected types.

preconditions: raw is a dict (caller must ensure this; if not a dict, safe default is returned)
postconditions: Return value is always a valid LLMResponseMeta instance
If raw is valid, returned instance reflects the parsed values
If raw is invalid or malformed, returned instance has ready_to_proceed=False and problem_model_update={}
No exception is raised regardless of input

functions.name: Session.touch
functions.description: Update the 'updated_at' timestamp to the current UTC time. Called whenever session state is modified to keep the persistence timestamp accurate.
preconditions: self is a valid Session instance
postconditions: self.updated_at is set to the current UTC time (datetime.now(timezone.utc))
self.updated_at >= the value of self.updated_at before the call
No other fields are modified

structured_side_effects.description: Updates the updated_at timestamp field to current UTC time.

functions.name: Session.to_json
functions.description: Serialize the full session state to a JSON string with 2-space indentation. Delegates to Pydantic's model_dump_json(indent=2). Suitable for writing to .constrain/sessions/<id>.json. Does NOT perform file I/O.

error_cases.name: serialization_failure
error_cases.condition: Pydantic serialization encounters an unserializable field value (should not happen with well-typed models)

preconditions: self is a valid Session instance with all required fields populated
postconditions: Returned string is valid JSON
JSON contains all Session fields including nested models
JSON is formatted with 2-space indentation
Roundtrip: Session.from_json(self.to_json()) produces an equivalent Session
Posture is serialized as its string value
Timestamps are serialized as ISO 8601 strings
UUID id is serialized as a string

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function — produces a string, no I/O.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is total size of conversation history and problem model

functions.name: Session.from_json
functions.description: Classmethod that deserializes a JSON string into a Session instance. Delegates to Pydantic's model_validate_json. Used when loading session state from .constrain/sessions/<id>.json. Does NOT perform file I/O.

inputs.name: raw
inputs.description: JSON string representing a serialized Session. Must be valid JSON conforming to the Session schema.

error_cases.name: schema_mismatch
error_cases.condition: JSON structure does not conform to the Session schema (missing required fields, wrong types)

error_cases.name: unknown_posture
error_cases.condition: Posture value in JSON is not a valid Posture enum member

error_data.message: Unknown posture: '{value}'. Valid values: adversarial, contrarian, critic, skeptic, collaborator

error_cases.name: unknown_phase
error_cases.condition: Phase value in JSON is not one of the valid Phase literals

error_data.message: Unknown phase: '{value}'. Valid values: understand, challenge, synthesize, complete

error_cases.name: unsupported_schema_version
error_cases.condition: schema_version in JSON is higher than the current supported version

postconditions: Returned Session is a fully valid instance with all fields populated
All nested models (Message, ProblemModel, Constraint, Artifacts) are properly deserialized
Posture is deserialized from its string value to the Posture enum member
Timestamps are deserialized to datetime objects
schema_version is preserved from the JSON

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function — parses a string, no I/O.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is total size of the JSON string

functions.name: Constraint.to_yaml_dict
functions.description: Export the constraint as a plain dict suitable for YAML serialization. Delegates to Pydantic's model_dump(mode='json') to ensure all values are JSON/YAML-safe primitives (no datetime objects, no enum instances — just strings and bools).
preconditions: self is a valid Constraint instance
postconditions: Returned dict contains keys: id, boundary, condition, violation, severity, rationale
All values are str (severity is the string 'must' or 'should', not an enum object)
Returned dict is safe for direct use with pyyaml dump

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function — produces a dict, no I/O.

invariants: models.py has zero internal project imports — it is the leaf dependency for all other components
All models are Pydantic BaseModel subclasses (except Posture which is enum.StrEnum and type aliases which are Literal)
All fields use type hints compatible with Python 3.12+
All list fields use Field(default_factory=list) — never mutable default arguments
All datetime fields default to datetime.now(timezone.utc) via default_factory — never mutable defaults
Session.id defaults to str(uuid4()) via default_factory — unique per instance
Posture enum members serialize to/from their string values (e.g., 'adversarial' not 'Posture.adversarial')
Phase, Status, Severity, and Role are Literal type aliases, not enum classes
ProblemModel.apply_update never raises on unknown keys — they are silently ignored
LLMResponseMeta.safe_parse never raises — returns safe defaults on any failure
Session serialization is roundtrip-stable: Session.from_json(session.to_json()) == session
schema_version field enables forward-compatible deserialization of persisted session files
Module total size target: approximately 200 lines or fewer
No file I/O in this module — serialization boundary stops at str/dict
